hero.author: By
hero.title: Zhatka — The Humanomics
hero.tagline: The traditional Indian way of slaughter — single stroke, minimum pain, maximum respect for life, animal rights, and Bharatiya honour.

whatIsJhatka.title: The Word is Self-Explanatory
whatIsJhatka.description: The word "Zhatka" literally means a strong, single stroke. Zhatka Meat refers to the traditional Indian method of slaughtering an animal for food in the least painful manner possible.
whatIsJhatka.highlight: Zhatka causes minimum pain and trauma to the animal. As pain is a message sent by the brain to the body — in Zhatka, that communication severs instantly. Science and humanity both approve.
whatIsJhatka.details: In a single powerful strike, the spinal cord is severed and the animal feels no prolonged pain. This is the ancient, humane, and scientifically approved method our ancestors practiced for centuries.

whyJhatka.title: 5 Reasons to Choose Jhatka
whyJhatka.reason1: The ancient Indian tradition of Jhatka is at the verge of extinction. Traditional Khatik butchers are being bulldozed by the Meat Mafia. We must preserve our heritage.
whyJhatka.reason2: We must defend the shrinking space of castes like KHATICS. Those who have been in this trade for ages face discrimination and unemployment.
whyJhatka.reason3: Slow and torturous killing leads to contamination. Stress hormones released during prolonged killing spoil the meat. Health-conscious people choose Jhatka only.
whyJhatka.reason4: Every animal has the right to die with the least possible pain. A single swift stroke severs all connections instantaneously — this is humanity's duty.
whyJhatka.reason5: Every consumer has the right to eat by their own choice. No seller or Meat Mafia can impose their personal belief or convenience on consumers.

events.title: Events & News
events.event1Desc: A major awareness event to educate people about the importance of Jhatka and the traditional Bhartiya way of life.
events.event2Desc: A gathering of Hindu, Sikh and Jain community members to discuss and preserve the tradition of Jhatka slaughter.
events.event3Desc: How the youth is taking up the cause of Jhatka and spreading awareness about Bhartiya food traditions across India.

video.title: Jhatka — The Truth
video.caption: Subscribe to our YouTube Channel — more videos on Jhatka, tradition & awareness

scripture.quote: "Distinguish between Meat and Meat, Cow and Goat, Jhatka and Halal. No alien food system can be forced upon Hindus and Bhartis."

appeals.title: A Message for Everyone
appeals.appeal1: Insist for Jhatka from your very next order — be it raw, cooked, or served in a hotel, train, airline, or gathering. Always ask: "Is this Jhatka?"
appeals.appeal2: Differentiate between killing and torturous killing. Oppose the imposition of any foreign food system on non-vegetarian Hindus. Stand with your community.
appeals.appeal3: Care for animal pain. Care for the sentiments of others. Care for your health. A system that causes less suffering is always the better choice.

malhar.title: Malhar Certification
malhar.description: Malhar Certification is the trusted platform that verifies Jhatka vendors. Every Malhar-certified vendor ensures meat is slaughtered according to Hindu tradition — fresh, clean, and uncontaminated.
malhar.strictVerificationDesc: Only vendors following Hindu tradition receive Malhar certification after thorough verification.
malhar.purityGuaranteedDesc: Fresh, hygienic meat. No saliva contamination. No mixing with other animal meat.

cta.title: Join the Fight for Jhatka
cta.description: Register with us. Spread awareness. Insist for Jhatka. Every order is a vote for tradition, animal rights, and Bhartiya honour.

footer.description: The fight for Zhatka — preserving Bhartiya tradition, animal rights, consumer rights and Khatik community livelihoods across India.
